L1.area: Portal content display and interactive community features

L2.description: An unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability exists where HTTP parameter keys from user-submitted form data are directly interpolated into SQL query strings without sanitization. The application's global input sanitization routine only escapes parameter values, not their keys, and a form processing endpoint accesses the raw POST data instead of the application's sanitized request array, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL through crafted parameter key names.

L3.area: Portal poll voting functionality and its supporting utility functions
L3.description: An unauthenticated SQL injection vulnerability exists where HTTP POST parameter keys from poll voting form submissions are directly interpolated into SQL query strings without sanitization. The poll voting endpoint directly accesses the raw POST data instead of the application's sanitized request array, and the global input sanitization routine only escapes parameter values, not their keys, allowing an attacker to inject arbitrary SQL through a crafted poll identifier.

ground_truth.reason: The PortalPollsVote function directly interpolates an unsanitized HTTP POST parameter key ($poll_id from $_POST['votes']) into SQL queries, enabling unauthenticated SQL injection via crafted votes parameter keys.
